text.title: Text Processing Tools - JSON, Regex, Diff & More
text.description: Free online text processing tools for developers. Format JSON, test regex, compare diffs, convert cases, and more. All tools run 100% in your browser.
text.intro: A suite of 9 text processing tools designed for developers who work with structured and unstructured text daily. Format and validate JSON, format and minify SQL, test regular expressions with real-time matching, compare text differences, write Markdown with live preview, convert text cases, extract emails and URLs, count words and characters, and remove duplicate lines — all without sending any data to a server.
text.faq1Q: What text processing tools does OmniKit offer?
text.faq1A: OmniKit provides 9 text processing tools: JSON Formatter (with JSON5 support), SQL Formatter, Regex Tester, Text Diff, Markdown Editor, Text Case Converter, Email & URL Extractor, Word Counter, and Deduplicate Lines.
text.faq2Q: Are these text processing tools free?
text.faq2A: Yes, all OmniKit text processing tools are completely free and run entirely in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
text.faq3Q: Can I use these tools with large files?
text.faq3A: Yes. Most tools handle files up to several MB. JSON Formatter, Diff, and Markdown Editor use Web Workers for smooth performance on large inputs.

encoding.title: Encoding & Conversion Tools - Base64, URL, CSV, YAML
encoding.description: Free online encoding and conversion tools for developers. Base64 encode/decode, URL encode/decode, CSV conversion, YAML conversion, number base conversion, and more. 100% client-side.
encoding.intro: 7 encoding and conversion tools that cover the most common data transformation needs. Encode and decode Base64 strings, URL-encode parameters, convert between JSON and CSV or YAML, transform number bases, and convert storage units. Every operation runs locally in your browser — no server uploads.
encoding.faq1Q: What encoding tools does OmniKit provide?
encoding.faq1A: OmniKit offers 7 encoding and conversion tools: Base64 Encoder/Decoder, URL Encoder/Decoder, JSON/CSV Converter, CSV/Markdown Table Converter, Number Base Converter, JSON/YAML Converter, and Storage Unit Converter.
encoding.faq2A: Yes. All conversions follow their respective RFC specifications (RFC 4648 for Base64, RFC 3986 for URL encoding) and produce standards-compliant output.

security.title: Security & Cryptography Tools - JWT, Hashing, Encryption
security.description: Free online security and cryptography tools for developers. Debug JWT tokens, generate hashes, create secure passwords, generate SSH keys, encrypt text, and calculate file checksums. 100% client-side.
security.intro: 6 security and cryptography tools for developers. Debug and verify JWT tokens, generate MD5/SHA hashes, create cryptographically secure passwords, generate SSH key pairs, encrypt and decrypt text with AES/DES/RC4, and calculate file checksums. All cryptographic operations run entirely in your browser — your data never leaves your device.
security.faq1Q: Are the security tools safe to use with sensitive data?
security.faq1A: Yes. All security tools run 100% client-side using JavaScript cryptographic libraries. No data is ever transmitted to any server. You can verify this by checking the open-source code.
security.faq2A: The Encrypt/Decrypt tool supports AES-256, DES, Triple DES, Rabbit, RC4, and RC4Drop. The Hash Generator supports MD5, SHA-1, SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, SHA3, Keccak, and RIPEMD-160.

generators.title: Generator Tools - UUID, Cron, Timestamp, QR Code
generators.description: Free online generator tools for developers. Generate UUIDs, build Cron expressions, convert Unix timestamps, and create QR codes. All tools run 100% in your browser.
generators.intro: 4 generator tools that help you create common developer artifacts. Generate UUID v4 and v7 identifiers, build and decode Cron expressions with next-run preview, convert Unix timestamps, and generate styled QR codes for text, URLs, WiFi, and more. Everything runs locally in your browser.
generators.faq1Q: What can I generate with OmniKit?
generators.faq1A: OmniKit provides 4 generator tools: UUID Generator (v4/v7), Cron Expression Generator, Unix Timestamp Converter, and QR Code Generator with custom styling.
generators.faq2A: Yes. UUID v4 uses cryptographically secure random numbers, and UUID v7 combines timestamp with randomness. Both conform to RFC 4122/9562.

visual.title: Visual & Media Tools - Color Converter, Image Compressor, PDF Merger
visual.description: Free online visual and media tools for developers. Convert colors, compress and convert images, merge PDFs. 100% client-side.
visual.intro: 5 visual and media tools for design, image, and document workflows. The Color Converter handles 7 color spaces with palette extraction and contrast checking. Image Resizer, Compressor, and Converter handle image transformations. PDF Merger combines multiple PDFs into one with drag-and-drop reordering. All processing runs locally in your browser.
visual.faq1A: The Color Converter supports HEX, RGB, HSL, HSV, CMYK, LAB, and OKLCH with bidirectional conversion and visual comparison.
visual.faq2Q: Is there a file size limit for image compression?
visual.faq2A: No. Image compression runs entirely in your browser using Canvas API, so there is no server-side file size limit. Very large images may be slower to process.

reference.title: Reference & Lookup Tools - HTTP Status, ASCII, HTML Codes, Subnet
reference.description: Free online reference and lookup tools for developers. HTTP status codes, ASCII table, HTML entities, SQLite viewer, HTTP client, and subnet calculator. All tools run 100% in your browser.
reference.intro: 6 reference and lookup tools for quick developer lookups. Browse HTTP status codes with RFC references, look up ASCII character codes, encode HTML entities, explore SQLite databases with a SQL editor, test REST APIs with the HTTP client, and calculate subnets with the IP Subnet Calculator. All tools are free and run entirely in your browser.
reference.faq1Q: What reference tools does OmniKit provide?
reference.faq1A: OmniKit offers 6 reference tools: HTTP Status Codes reference, HTTP Client for API testing, SQLite Database Viewer, ASCII Table, HTML Entity Encoder, and IP Subnet Calculator.
reference.faq2Q: Can I run SQL queries in the DB Viewer?
reference.faq2A: Yes. The SQLite Viewer supports read-only SQL queries with autocomplete, query history, pagination, and export to CSV or JSON — all running locally in your browser via WebAssembly.

workflows.title: Data Pipeline & Workflow Tools - Recipe Builder, Batch Processor
workflows.description: Free online data pipeline and workflow tools for developers. Chain operations into recipes, or apply a single step to multiple inputs with Batch Processor. 100% client-side.
workflows.intro: 2 workflow tools: Recipe chains multiple operations into a data processing pipeline, and Batch Processor applies a single operation to many inputs at once. Both run entirely in your browser.
workflows.faq1A: Recipe is a data pipeline builder that lets you chain multiple operations together. For example, you can Base64-encode text, then hash the result, then convert to uppercase — all in one flow.
workflows.faq2A: Batch Processor applies a single processing step — like hashing, encoding, or compression — to multiple inputs simultaneously. Paste text or drop files, pick a step, and get all results instantly.
